title: veronica serpyllifolia var. humifusa
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia var. humifusa, also known as creeping speedwell, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with small, blue-purple flowers that thrive in moist, shady areas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of creeping speedwell have been found to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs in salves and ointments to leverage its medicinal properties.
